@@ -40,7 +40,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3968","title":"Task: E2E test - Mobile viewport interactions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:48:17.043234743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T00:16:59.548383147Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T00:16:59.548383147Z","close_reason":"Covered in homepage.spec.ts (Responsive Layout tests) and prompt-detail.spec.ts (Mobile Bottom Sheet tests) - tests mobile viewport shows correct layout, touch-friendly buttons, and bottom sheet behavior on 390x844 viewport with hasTouch enabled","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3968","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:58:11.341337517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3b05","title":"Task: Create TestLogger class with levels (debug/info/step/error)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:46:26.173743647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T23:22:20.532200200Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T23:22:20.532200200Z","close_reason":"Created TestLogger class with debug/info/step/error levels, timestamps, context formatting, and file output for CI artifacts","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3b05","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-50cn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:54:52.182623225Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3fms","title":"Add visual separation between Featured and Browse sections","description":"# Task: Clarify Featured vs Browse Section Boundary ## Problem Statement Currently, the Featured section appears immediately after the Hero, followed by the filter controls and browse grid. This creates confusion because: 1. **Filters don't affect featured**: User applies a filter, sees 6 featured prompts unchanged, thinks filtering is broken 2. **No visual boundary**: Featured and Browse blend together 3. **Duplicated prompts**: Featured prompts may appear again in the browse grid ## Current Layout ``` Hero (search + category pills) ↓ Featured Prompts (6 cards, unaffected by filters) ↓ Filter Controls (Category, Tags, Clear) ↓ Results Header ("All Prompts" / "Search Results") ↓ Browse Grid (filtered results) ``` ## Proposed Solution ### Option A: Move Featured Above Filters, Add Clear Separator ```tsx {/* Hero */} {/* Featured Section - clearly labeled, visually distinct */}

Featured Prompts

Curated by the team
{/* Clear visual break */}
{/* Browse Section - with filters */}

Browse All Prompts

``` ### Option B: Integrate Featured into Results Remove separate featured section. Instead: - Add "Featured" badge to featured prompts in the grid - Sort featured prompts to top by default - Filters affect all prompts including featured ```tsx // In PromptGrid or page.tsx const sortedPrompts = useMemo(() => { // Featured first, then the rest const featured = filteredPrompts.filter(p => p.featured); const nonFeatured = filteredPrompts.filter(p => !p.featured); return [...featured, ...nonFeatured]; }, [filteredPrompts]); ``` ### Option C: Collapsible Featured Section (Best of Both) Show featured by default, allow collapsing: ```tsx const [showFeatured, setShowFeatured] = useState(true);
Featured Prompts {featuredCount}
``` ## Recommended Approach **Option A** is simplest and clearest: - Distinct visual sections with clear labels - Gradient separator creates elegant break - "Curated by the team" explains why they're special - Users understand filters apply only to "Browse" section ## Implementation Details 1. Add section wrapper with border-bottom to FeaturedPromptsSection 2. Add "Browse All Prompts" heading above filter controls 3. Add subtle gradient divider between sections 4. Consider adding "(not affected by filters)" note near Featured heading ## Testing 1. Apply category filter - featured section unchanged, browse section filtered 2. Verify visual separation is clear in both light/dark modes 3. Screen reader: sections are announced as distinct regions 4. Mobile: sections stack correctly ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/app/page.tsx - apps/web/src/components/landing/FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx (possibly) ## Dependencies - Should be done after color consistency (page.tsx) - Affects layout, may need responsive testing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:47:27.281567949Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T07:46:09.758005579Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T07:46:09.758005579Z","close_reason":"Implemented Option A: Added 'Featured Prompts' title with 'Curated by the team' subtitle, border-bottom on Featured section, gradient divider between sections, 'Browse All Prompts' section heading, and contextual results count. Build passes.","compaction_level":0} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3jiy","title":"Implement moderation queue prioritization","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-0om4. ## Task Prioritize moderation queue based on urgency and impact. ## Priority Factors - Report count (more reports = higher priority) - Reporter reputation (trusted reporters boost priority) - Content author tier (premium users slightly lower priority for false reports) - Report reason severity (illegal > harassment > spam) - Time in queue (aging boost) ## Features - Auto-calculated priority score - Priority badges in queue - Filter by priority level - SLA tracking (time to review) - Auto-escalation for stale items ## Database Changes - Add priority_score to reported_content - Add escalated_at, reviewed_by - Create priority calculation function ## UI - Priority column with color coding - Sort by priority (default) - Priority breakdown on hover - SLA countdown indicator ## Success Criteria - Critical items reviewed first - Fair and consistent scoring - SLA compliance tracking - Escalation notifications ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:17:02.161025256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:17:02.161025256Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3jiy","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fhci","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:29.400235563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3jiy","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-kk7l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:29.527931921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3jiy","title":"Implement moderation queue prioritization","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-0om4. ## Task Prioritize moderation queue based on urgency and impact. ## Priority Factors - Report count (more reports = higher priority) - Reporter reputation (trusted reporters boost priority) - Content author tier (premium users slightly lower priority for false reports) - Report reason severity (illegal > harassment > spam) - Time in queue (aging boost) ## Features - Auto-calculated priority score - Priority badges in queue - Filter by priority level - SLA tracking (time to review) - Auto-escalation for stale items ## Database Changes - SLA countdown indicator ## Success Criteria - Critical items reviewed first - Fair and consistent scoring - SLA compliance tracking - Escalation notifications ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:17:02.161025256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T06:36:20.407784189Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3jiy","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fhci","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:29.400235563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3jiy","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-kk7l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:29.527931921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3kx","title":"Implement jfp suggest command","description":"Part 14.2 of PLAN. 'jfp suggest ' suggests relevant prompts for a task. Uses BM25 search by default. Supports --semantic flag for optional semantic rerank. --json output for agents. Shows prompt relevance scores and brief descriptions. Helps users discover prompts for their current task.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:46:32.384175484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T07:12:15.044824264Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T07:12:15.044824264Z","close_reason":"Implemented jfp suggest with BM25 search, relevance bars, and contextual tips","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3kx","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xdt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:49:17.040792653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3kx","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-z16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:49:17.014042309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3plh","title":"Task: CategoryFilter component unit tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"agent-claude","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:44:52.601116655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T00:38:29.805660659Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T00:38:29.805660659Z","close_reason":"Created 16 unit tests for CategoryFilter component covering rendering, interactions, accessibility, and edge cases.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3plh","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wxw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:55:12.299518354Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3q8a","title":"Implement keyboard shortcuts for power users","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-4us. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Implement keyboard shortcuts for power users. ## Why This Matters Power users expect keyboard navigation. Good keyboard shortcuts: - Speed up common workflows - Improve accessibility - Create muscle memory for frequent actions ## Keyboard Shortcuts ### Global Shortcuts | Shortcut | Action | Context | |----------|--------|---------| | `Cmd/Ctrl + K` | Open search | Global | | `Cmd/Ctrl + /` | Show shortcuts help | Global | | `Cmd/Ctrl + N` | Create new prompt | Global | | `Escape` | Close modal/panel | When open | | `G then H` | Go to home/dashboard | Navigation | | `G then L` | Go to library | Navigation | | `G then S` | Go to Swap Meet | Navigation | ### Editor Shortcuts | Shortcut | Action | Context | |----------|--------|---------| | `Cmd/Ctrl + S` | Save/submit | In editor | | `Cmd/Ctrl + Enter` | Save and close | In editor | | `Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + P` | Toggle preview | In editor | ### List/Grid Shortcuts | Shortcut | Action | Context | |----------|--------|---------| | `J` / `K` | Move down/up | In list | | `Enter` | Open selected | In list | | `S` | Save/bookmark | On item | | `C` | Copy prompt | On item | | `/` | Focus search | In list | ### Navigation Shortcuts | Shortcut | Action | |----------|--------| | `[` / `]` | Previous/next page | | `?` | Show keyboard help | ## Implementation ### Keyboard Hook ```typescript // src/hooks/use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts import { useHotkeys } from 'react-hotkeys-hook'; export function useKeyboardShortcuts() { const router = useRouter(); // Global navigation useHotkeys('g h', () => router.push('/dashboard')); useHotkeys('g l', () => router.push('/library')); useHotkeys('g s', () => router.push('/swap-meet')); // Search useHotkeys('mod+k', (e) => { e.preventDefault(); openSearchModal(); }); // Help useHotkeys('shift+/', () => openShortcutsHelp()); } ``` ### Shortcuts Help Modal Display all shortcuts in an organized modal: - Grouped by context - Show current platform keys (Cmd vs Ctrl) - Search shortcuts ### Visual Indicators - Show shortcut hints in tooltips - Keyboard icon badge on buttons - Shortcut in dropdown menu items ## Dependencies ```json { "react-hotkeys-hook": "^4.0.0" } ``` ## Accessibility - Don't override screen reader shortcuts - Provide non-keyboard alternatives - Announce actions to screen readers ## Files to Create - `src/hooks/use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts` - `src/components/keyboard-shortcuts-help.tsx` - `src/lib/shortcuts.ts` (shortcut definitions) ## Success Criteria - All shortcuts work as documented - No conflicts with browser/OS shortcuts - Help modal is discoverable - Shortcuts work across all browsers ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:18:14.303276899Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T07:59:45.057505925Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T07:59:45.057505925Z","close_reason":"Wired global keyboard shortcuts and help modal (search, help, nav, new prompt)","compaction_level":0}
